title: Post CABG breathlessness follow-up
text: Patient admitted on 08/04/2026 after CABG performed 6 days ago. At 10:30 AM, patient developed SOB with SpO2 89%. Furosemide 40 mg IV was administered at 10:45 AM. Oxygen started at 2 L/min and SpO2 improved to 95% by 11:15 AM. ECG showed no new ischemic changes.

title: Diabetes outpatient glucose management
text: Known type 2 diabetes mellitus on metformin 500 mg twice daily. GRBS recorded at 298 mg/dL on 09/04/2026 at 07:10 AM. Sliding-scale insulin 6 units SC was administered. Repeat capillary blood glucose at 09:15 AM was 232 mg/dL.

title: Community acquired pneumonia treatment note
text: Fever with productive cough for 3 days and mild dyspnea. Chest X-ray suggests right lower lobe infiltrate. Started ceftriaxone 1 g IV once daily and azithromycin 500 mg oral. Blood pressure remained stable through the shift.

title: Acute wheeze and nebulization response
text: Episode of wheeze and breathlessness at 21:05. Nebulized salbutamol 2.5 mg with ipratropium was given. Respiratory rate reduced from 30/min to 20/min within 25 minutes. No chest pain reported after treatment.

title: Post-operative DVT prophylaxis protocol
text: Post-operative DVT prophylaxis protocol recommends enoxaparin 40 mg SC once daily. Hold dose in active bleeding or severe renal dysfunction. Continue early mobilization and hydration monitoring.
